senses.definition: relating to or used in a referendum or the process of referring a matter to voters.
examples: The referendal process requires a public vote.
They debated the referendal aspects of the proposal.

senses.definition: plural of referendar y; individuals serving as or pertaining to a referendar,y (historical/clerical role related to reference or reference duties in certain jurisdictions).
examples: The referendaries advised on legal references.
Several referendaries were appointed to assist the council.

senses.definition: a person who has the office or function of a referendary; an official who issues or deals with references, often in historical or governmental contexts.
examples: The referendary maintained records of decrees.
A senior referendary prepared the documents for the king.

senses.definition: the position, rank, or duties of a referendary; the office of an official who handles reference or reference-related tasks.
examples: Her referendaryship included archive management.
During the era, referendaryship carried significant administrative authority.

senses.definition: a general vote by the electorate on a single political question put to them by the government.
examples: The referendum decided the country's future.
They held a referendum on the constitutional change.

senses.definition: plural of referendum; votes by the electorate on political questions.
examples: Many referendums have shaped the nation’s laws.
Referendums vary in turnout and impact.

senses.definition: the thing a word or phrase stands for; an object or concept that a term refers to.
examples: In linguistics, the referent of 'dog' is a real animal.
Context helps identify the referent in a sentence.

senses.definition: relating to references or indicating what a word or phrase refers to in context.
examples: The text is highly referential to ancient myths.
Referential language points to the objects discussed.

senses.definition: plural of referentiality; the qualities of being referential or the ways in which language refers to things.
examples: The referentialities of the text reveal its themes.
Scholars study referentialities across languages.

senses.definition: the quality of referring to something or describing the way language refers to objects, people, or ideas.
examples: Referentiality shapes how readers interpret characters.
The referentiality of pronouns affects clarity.

senses.definition: in a way that refers to or denotes something in language or text.
examples: The term is used referentially to point to the concept.
She focuses referentially on earlier chapters.

senses.definition: in a referent-related way; with reference to something.
examples: The phrase is used referently in this context.
Referential cues are used referently to guide interpretation.

senses.definition: plural of referent; things or concepts that a word or phrase stands for.
examples: Pronouns replace nouns and refer to their referents.
Different contexts can change the referents of a sentence.

senses.definition: historical term for something referred or returned to; in law, a formal remand or reversion of a decision.
examples: The referment of the case required new evidence.
In medieval law, referment procedures existed in some jurisdictions.

senses.definition: capable of being referred to or directed to someone for help or information; able to be referred to a referral.
examples: This issue is referrable to the specialist.
Any case that is referrable can be forwarded to the committee.

senses.definition: an act of directing someone to another person or organization for help or information; a mention or source of business.
examples: A referral to a specialist was made.
The clinic keeps a list of referrals.

senses.definition: plural of referral; instances of directing someone to another source or recommending someone.
examples: They track referrals to measure outreach success.
Referrals from existing customers boosted growth.

senses.definition: past tense of refer; to mention or direct someone to a source, or to send to another person for help.
examples: She was referred to a specialist.
The document was referred to the committee.

senses.definition: a person who refers someone to a person or service; a source of a referral.
examples: The doctor is a referrer for specialist services.
Your referrer can help you get a faster appointment.

senses.definition: plural of referrer; people who refer others to a service or professional.
examples: Referrers received a thank-you note.
The program tracks referrals from multiple referrers.

senses.definition: capable of being referred to or directed to someone for information or help.
examples: The case is referrible to the appropriate department.
Only referrible issues should be submitted.

senses.definition: the quality of being referable or able to be referred; degree of suitability for referral.
examples: High referribleness means quicker routing to specialists.
Assess the referribleness of each submission.

senses.definition: present participle of refer; to mention or direct someone to a source or person for help.
examples: She is referring the case to a specialist.
The article is referring to earlier findings.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of refer; to mention or direct to a source or person.
examples: This article refers to previous studies.
He refers patients to a specialist.

senses.definition: capable of being fertilized again or made fertile again; biologically or metaphorically capable of renewal.
examples: In theory, the soil is refertilizable after restoration.
The field is refertilizable with proper nutrients.
